reviews.review_text: Very personable and helpful managers, and excellent location at Bright Accommodation Park G'day. Crisp linen on beds, good quality towels, and nice kitchen china and cookware. Exceptionally clean cabins (some needing an urgent refurb). Good camp sites with views, 2 modern pod cabins with own bathrooms a good alternative to tents.

Some of the facilities are a bit old, but they're all well maintained. Dorm style rooms recently renovated and well appointed. Onsite manager is only a phone call or text away. Great little place. Only downside is distance to town.
Cabin was clean and tidy and everything worked.
Good cabin could not say anything wrong about it

Great location as its a little bit out of Bright. The place has charm with lots of little play areas for the kids. Managers are always available if you need any help. Very much enjoyed our stay and look forward to returning 💛

We camped on a powered site with a caravan
The camping area is rustic but tidy, the park  definitely lives up to its “crusty” moniker but the rural surroundings could be preferable to the sterile environment of some other parks. Facilities are old but clean and functioning.
If you want a fire, BYO pit and wood. Manager Colin was very friendly and accommodating for us and we enjoyed our 3 night stay

We recently stayed at this caravan park and had a really enjoyable experience from start to finish. The grounds were clean, well-maintained, and had a relaxed, welcoming atmosphere that made it easy to switch off and unwind.

The amenities were a standout — modern, spotless, and clearly looked after regularly. The camp kitchen was especially handy, with everything we needed, and the BBQ areas were perfect for a laid-back evening meal.

Staff were friendly and helpful without being overbearing, and check-in was smooth and hassle-free. It’s always a good sign when a place runs efficiently but still feels personal.

The location was also a big plus, close enough to local shops and attractions while still being quiet and peaceful at night. Whether you’re travelling solo, as a couple, or with family, it’s a great spot to base yourself.

Overall, a really pleasant stay and somewhere we’d happily return to. Highly recommended.
Great place, management were extremely helpful and friendly. Showers and toilets very clean.

A very nice and clean place that is conveniently located. A good choice if you are traveling with pets. Enjoyed the stay. A full kitchen was a great inclusion as we were traveling with twin toddlers and a cavoodle. Highly recommend the place.

We had a really nice time at this caravan park!
Everyone was so lovely & respectful of others around them, you can tell there was a great social aspect there.
We stayed at a powered site, the ONLY downside was that there was a light between lot 81 & 80 that is quite bright so does shine in to your tent.
Showers and toilets were all clean, water was great. 3 parks and lots of roads for our kids to ride their bikes on.
We also loved the location as opposed to smack bang in the middle of Bright as the roads were so busy and other caravan parks looked super crowded.
Will definitely be back.
A beautiful clean caravan park . Unfortunately my stay was brief, but I will stay for longer next time

The Buffalo Cabin.
Strong musty odour, very old outdated cabin.
Putrid raggety couch cover with an even more disgusting single cushion on it.

The sheet on the bed was filthy, as if someone has slept in it for 100 consecutive nights without bathing.

Despite sleeping in my clothes and wearing my hair wrapped to avoid direct contact, imagine my delight to wake up with bedbug bites on my body.

The park itself is lacking.
Its very 1970s, the bathroom block was not at all a pleasant bathing area.
Think "cinder blocks and dark".

The term "treetop rooms" is misleading.
The tree top rooms look like an abandoned long forgotten thought, best described as a room above and an old garage.. circa 1970s.

The park is out of town, which is really here, nor there... however, the reception kiosk offers nothing. Its a portable tiny room that appears to be a storage room.

There's no on-site food and beverage options, not a loaf of bread or coffee insight.

The park has so much potential, to be levelled and developed to be an oasis for outdoor enthusiasts and families exploring the area.
Booked a mountain view pod and too be honest I am quite disappointed. For what you pay not worth it at all. Still have to walk to old bathroom which was not advertised on website anywhere.
Cabins were clean but very pokey. Could barely move in the shower and water heat was erratic. Some of the furniture was in sub-standard condition.

We stayed in a premium powered camp site in our caravan for 3 nights with no provision for waste water. The amenities were the worst I’ve seen , no hot water for showers, no lights in shower area , only one lockable toilet door in the ladies. All this for $68 per night , that’s a premium cost for a very disappointing experience… unless they make some serious changes I would stay anywhere else .

We were in our caravan. A nice grassed site. Amenities were tired and in need of updating, but all worked well. The walk in to town is possible, but a long way! The host came to greet us and was very friendly.
It is an older park but well maintained & very clean. Lovely location nestled in the trees

DO NOT stay here! We booked a family room which we were in for about an hour before leaving to find somewhere else. Bugs everywhere in the room and when my husband pulled the sheets back a white tail spider crawled out of the bed. Then had another one crawling on his leg  twenty mins later (photo attached). The lady working there was rude and wouldn’t give us another room because it was a “two night minimum stay”. She did offer to make the room up again which had me thinking how long ago was the room actually made up. The fact this is called a family room is a disgrace- no fridge (not sure how you’re meant to keep medication, breastmilk bottles etc cool), no tv - not even a box of tissues in the room. Was overpriced at $129 a room. Communal toilets and bathrooms are downstairs and would not want to be going down there in the night. Avoid at all costs, much better places to stay at!!!

If your ideal getaway is to have whitetail spiders crawling across your legs then this place is for you. Treetop rooms are basically shitty dirty room with beds jammed inside, toilets are quite a ways down stairs and we're probably installed sometime in the 70s. I have no idea where the kitchen was supposed to be. We decided to leave instantly when someone from the park said, we can't give you another room as it's a 2 night minimum, but there are other better places in Bright that aren't much more in price and are far better. We ended up paying for another room rather than drive 3.5 hours home. It's smet in the country is not answer to why there are so many spiders inside

We booked a treetop twin room to break our journey during a heatwave, and it was exactly what we needed. Rooms were very clean, air-con was lovely on a 42 degree day. Grounds were well kept although the shared toilets are showing their age. Nice and quiet, lots of bird sounds. Would stay again!

My husband and I stayed for a couple of nights. The park was quiet, except for a couple of brief stints when kids on bikes were using the hill at the back of the park as their personal mountain bike trail and then there was one afternoon when the person living on the adjoining property did several hours of drum practise. The correspondence before getting to the park clearly indicated that they the people running the park didn't want anything to do with us and we didn't speak or see anyone in the time we were there that you could've asked a question of if you wanted to (was reminiscent of Covid times, awful). The toilets were ok but the showers, despite the signs asking us to limit our showers to 2-3 mins, I think I spend much more time than that trying to get a consistent temperature. Would probably free camp before returning to this park.

Seemingly an ok park, if on the expensive side for the location, but spoiled by the large number of those not legitimately at the park hanging around thieving and doing illicit drug deals.
Not helped by those in fake park shirts trying to gain access to rooms, as was the case with mine last night. More attention needs to be shown to who should and shouldn't be here, and action taken to remove the undesirables.
One word. Amazing. I highly recommend staying at this park

I booked this last minute and was so happy to  book a cabin for my family in the peak season. The cabin was well equipped and tidy. Location is great with only a quick 5 minute drive to town. Workers are friendly and accommodating.

We originally booked a room that didn’t suit us - totally our fault.

When we called Michael about it he was quick to listen to the type of room we wanted and found it for us. I was impressed with this because he didn’t have to do anything.

Our kids enjoyed the pool and playground and it was quiet and peaceful.

We reached out for assistance a few times during our stay and Michael and Samantha were always happy to help. Their service was awesome.

Thanks guys!

Thanks BAP for an incredible stay. This is our second year coming here, the cabins are well equipped, clean and comfy! Our kids have such a great time coming here and its great to see the new playgrounds you've added!
Thanks BAP for such a fun accommodating time. See you next year!

Our family and friends have been coming here for 3 years now and we absolutely love it. Clean cabins, great playground and pool for the kids and a beautiful walk into Bright along the bike path. Thanks Samantha and crew - we can't wait to come back next year!

The office is not open . A phone call will be answered by a person changing the cabin linen. The pool is cloudy and doesn’t appear to be properly maintained. The amenities are tired and need resurfacing . The place is green and shady and a beautiful place to stay- the caravan/ camping sites are generous . It’s a long walk into Bright along the rail trail - it’s lovely

Nice and big sites with grass in January
No dump point found and none on the map
Each afternoon the grey water was pumped out into the ditch just behind our caravan. It stunk

Michael the very handsome man from reception, was prompt to help us with our various issues, his service greatly enhanced our stay at this wonderful park. Overall the facilities were clean and the location is brilliant. Would definitely stay here again.

Camped at a standard powered site for 4 nights over Christmas. The standard powered sites are extremely rocky, which is not mentioned anywhere on the website when booking online. All my tent pegs bent due to the hard surface. We were lucky it wasn’t windy, otherwise our tents could have ended up in Bright town. Would definitely choose a premium powered site next time. Everything else was perfect

Great place, great location, a few km out of Bright, but a bike track to be able to ride into Bright. Room was lovely and clean, well maintained, with deck facing pool opposite.
The bed was too hard for my body, but that is a personal preference.
Great park just out of town very n8ce clean cabins pool is perfect for hot days
Unfortunately your non contact check in and check out left us disappointed as we like to be welcomed by and meet the owners/staff.

Great park
Well kept sites and facilities
Short ride on the bike path to town and the pub. But quieter than the big park in town which was great.
Awesome staff we will definitely be back

The living room was the best part of the cabins - nice and spacious. Good for playing games with family and friends
Pretty quiet at the time of year when we visited (mid November). The cleaning in the cabins were quite bad, a lot of surfaces not cleaned at all with crumbs still on it. The linen had brown or black marks on them - some was definitely dirt, not sure if they were even washed from previous stays (we had two different cabins - one with private bathroom and one with shared bathroom) and both rooms had the same issues with linen. It’s quite disgusting.
There was also no liquid detergent used to wash dishes and we had been told to clean up the cabins after ourselves. Not sure how everyone else washed their dishes with no soap and we couldn’t either. We could only rinse with hot water. Not hygienic at all
A bit expensive for a powered site and no toilet dump for caravans. Sites were large and quiet.

Way overpriced for a family dorm-type room only when fully self sufficient cabin is almost the same price. Beds & pillows are very uncomfortable with a mattress on a high wooden platform you need to jump up on to access. For a family room consisting of a double bed & two singles it should at least have a small TV on the wall for the kids & a small bar fridge for drinks, milk medications, etc. Split system wasn't working on the first night so opened latch to check the filter & all the dead moths & bugs fell onto the bed - filter was disgusting & never been cleaned. Room smelt mouldy. Not a pleasant stay
Our powered caravan site was grassy, plenty of room and super quiet. Being able to have a fire was a bonus and the pool was a hit.
We found the accommodation quiet clean and comfortable
I stayed for a night and was very pleased with their services. They upgraded me to a larger room for a simple mishap, which was a very generous gesture. Indeed, coming back and recommending.

Wonderful location outside the main town. Very quiet and lovely walk into Bright.
Cosy cabin, Hotham, with comfy bed and good shower.
A couch was needed but would be a tight fit.
No one there for checkin but details sent to my email.
Great for having a dog travel with you. Lovely relaxed feel to the park
A larger table and maybe a microwave in the TT rooms would be useful. There was no hot water in the men's showers on the first morning I was there. Otherwise, a nice park with good amenities.
We were in a 2 room (6 people) cabin. Although it was a little squeezy when everyone was there it had everything that we needed. We also had our dog which was great.
The rooms are as described on their website
The new owners are GREAT!
Enjoying the comfort of a cabin at Bright Accomodation Park when temperatures are a bit too low for tent camping. The Bright Accomodation Park cabins provide everything you need at a good location.

Avoid - the linen were not changed on all 3 beds. We had to sleep with clothes on, as the bedsheets were that gross.

Management said sorry, but did not offer refund.
Little piece of paradise
The room stunk of oven cleaner the whole stay and mold in the bathrooms.

Finding accommodation in bright isn’t necessarily cheap. This was the most affordable option and I love that they have something for everyone here. It’s a great little location. It’s a short drive into town. We would definitely return.

I stayed one night at Bright Accommodation Park in one of their cabins and was very pleased with the experience. Although it’s a small park, it really delivers in terms of quality and service.
The cabin was clean, comfortable, and well-maintained — exactly what you want for a short stay. The location is excellent, offering easy access to the town and nearby attractions, yet it still feels quiet and relaxed.
What really stood out was the service from the staff. They were friendly, welcoming, and genuinely helpful, which made the stay even more enjoyable.
If you're looking for a clean, well-located place with great customer service in Bright, I’d definitely recommend this park.

Amazing park with a special shout out to top-notch service! It's so good that my wife insisted I leave this review.

Great place!
We had a wonderful stay fresh clean blankets sheets and towel made a big difference!
I stayed during Winter 2025 with my children. The cabin was super clean, staff were friendly and the accommodation was nice and warm. I will stay here again and highly recommend to others.

Caravan PArk with self-check-in upon registration,
Clean and hot shower
Good before to go Hotham skiing
will bw back next days
Not bad, solid accom for snow trips

Have stayed at this park two times now with more trips planned in the future. Each time has been great! Great facilities and constant new additions to keep improving the park. Definitely worth stopping in during your travels. Got to meet the park manager Sam and was shown unmatched hospitality. Looking forward to staying again!
Nice and quiet. Great walking tracks.
Thanks for postal my belonging back to me without any cost issue. Aircon so warm and nice hot water for shower. All staff so kind.

Absolutely incredible service here from the site manager! Helped us with all enquiries promptly and assisted us greatly with special requests. Would recommend to anyone who is having a getaway in the area to falls creek or Hotham!
It was a very cramped cabin for 5 people. We had noisy neighbours into late night and of course with no onsite manager they just got away with keeping us all awake.

We had a quick stay here on our way to the mountains. Easy access, communication was great, rooms warm & comfortable. We found the ammenities clean and in good service. We had a dorm style room, may have done with some earplugs as I’m a light sleeper but otherwise a good night.
poorly managed place. Dirty floor, rusted water kettle
We stayed in a 2 single bed room - very nice and clean, warm with a new heater, no noise - shared bathroom & shower were also very clean - excellent value for a ski trip
It was really great experience and enjoyed a lot. Really appreciate for everything,  cabins warm, neat and clean.
It was very nice. Really have a good time.

We recently stayed at Bright Accommodation Park and had a fantastic experience. The park is set in a peaceful, scenic location surrounded by nature and close to the town centre,

Highly recommended for families, couples, or anyone looking to enjoy the beauty of Bright. We’ll definitely be back!
Room was clean. Full kitchen and pretty decent for the cost. Would go again.

Rooms are ok. beds are good and bedding is also fine. more than worth the price paid. book from website only. on airbnb its for 440 and website its for 220. villas are good and everything works. AC was working but it was too cold so it was not enough. Family friendly and ample parking space

Thanks for an awesome overnighter! We were warm and super comfortable. Visiting the snow was super easy as the park is close to all the major attractions. We also went fishing at the ovens river, another close attraction.

Some complimentary toiletries would have been nice, especially when paying over $180 for one nights accommodation in a below average unit.
Hand rails on the front steps would be handy for those of us who are physically challenged.
Unit was dated, kitchen cupboards need attention, whole thing needs a serious update, not worth the price.
Frying pan was dirty and had to be washed before use. Bunk beds directly opposite the toilet - just no.
Only saving grace was a decent hot shower.

Great location, well set out park. Was a bit hard knowing what to do with grey water on our powered site. Hard to find someone to ask. Office not well attended even though I appreciate it's low season, and I was the only van there. Having said that I would go back.

My friends and I stayed in the treetop dorm style accom. The rooms were very clean and cosy and it was great having heating on a really cold night. The rooms have fresh carpet and paint which made a big difference to the vibe.
The bbq area/camp kitchen had everything we needed to cook which was great because we were on a bike trip and didn’t have any equipment.
It’s not as convenient or as nice as the one in town but it was still great for what we needed. Only downside is for the dorm rooms is that you have to walk outside and down stairs to get to the shared bathrooms, so be prepared for a cold trip to the toilet/shower.

I was not sure whether I wanted a cabin or powered site but glad we went with powered site at Bright Accommodation Park. Had water and two power points, nice soft ground. Bathrooms are clean and really appreciate the attention to detail to make it comfortable eg lots of hooks! The shared bbq area near the cabins has a fridge, microwave, kettle, sink, cutlery, crockery etc. Our family members went with the treetop cabin and it seemed like brand new! Would definitely recommend for anyone including families.
Only thing we didn't like was the barking dog at a property near the powered sites at night and the sink in women's bathroom clogging sometimes.
Will be back again!! Thank you :)

Unfortunately the heater in our room was not working though it was to be repaired the day after we left.  They did supply us with a small oil heater which was not quite adequate.  the mattress was also rather hard though we are older and probably would have been fine for a younger couple.  overall the staff were pleasant and surroundings nice.

aircon doesn't function properly.
electrical blanket doesn't work fully.
Rooms were nothing like the standard of the pictures. Beds were so so. Not even a lounge to lie on. We returned after 1 night of our 4 night stay.

We stayed in a Buffalo cabin, and were very disapointed. We intended to do our own cooking, but there was one sausepan which appeared to have boiled dry at some point, also, there was the top half of a steamer which was completely useless. There was a frying pan that appeared to have some food on the side, but we were able to use it after a good scrubbing.
The air conditioner only ran for about 5 minutes, then shut down. Staff supplied us with another small heater, which was some help on the cold nights.
Overall, we considered that staying in these cabins represented very poor value for money. The one redeeming feature was the staff who were very helpful.
Beautiful quiet location

Great bungalow - very clean, fully equipped kitchen and small but functional leaving area.
Bed was very comfortable and we had a fantastic stay overall.
Remarkably generous caravan sites made things very comfortable. 10 min bike ride on the bike track puts you close but out of the tourist bustle of town. Good price too.
Camped here and facilities were very nice. Showers were hot. The kitchen area had a communal fridge.

Everything was awful.
- cabins so close to the road made it noisy every night and the lack of fencing and boom gates made us constantly worry about our kids riding their bikes and potentially getting hit by a car driving in.
- cabin was dirty upon arrival, couch smelt like cigarettes and utensils were dirty
- electric blanket was set on high on both sides of the bed without our knowledge so when we turned the power point on to charge our phones the beds automatically came on. Woke up sweating to a burning hot bed at 11.30pm. Luckily no fire started.
- toilet leaked and smelt like urine though out the whole bathroom.
- the “park” was so small my children didn’t even want to play with it as they thought it was “someone’s they brought from home”
Cabins were so small and outdated, made for a very unpleasant experience. We booked for Friday - Monday and left first thing Sunday morning, didn’t want to stay longer than we had to.
Very disappointing stay
Quiet and relaxing.  Pool and complimentary bikes were a nice touch, beds comfy and communal kitchen area is very well set up for travellers.

Look, I can't fault the service because the manager was responsive and helpful when we asked for help getting the other campers to adhere to the 10pm noise curfew.

Unfortunately the site itself is also right next to a very noisy road that has cars driving 80km so there's no "peace and quiet".

The bathrooms are quite run down and in desperate need of upgrades. There is limited kitchen facilities (only one washing up station) and there was a very strong smell of sewerage the entire time we stayed over Easter weekend.

I personally won't be returning any time soon, but for unfussy campers it's got the basics that you'll need.

First time camping in Bright and would highly recommend the site. Everyone there was very considerate - seemed like repeat visitors to both the cabins and sites which is always a good sign. Great for kids and families!
You might get a cold shower and find all the sinks blocked

We had a powered site for a few nights and found this location ideal. It was out of town enough to be quieter on the roads and close enough to pop in for food and drinks. The site was grassy but plenty of space. It’s an older park than the one on the way to Wandiligong. I was at the newer park last year and found it too noisy and this one was much quieter. The amenities were old but clean. Staff were lovely whenI spoke to her and the process to getting your site details was emailed first thing on the day of arrival so you didn’t need to wait around to do check in.
I will definitely come back another time. Thanks guys! I look forward to next time!

We thoroughly enjoyed our 4 day stay at the Bright Accommodation Park. Although the cabin was smallish, it was comfortable,  all appliances ( TV, r/c conditioner, toaster, kettle etc.) worked well. The cabin was clean, except for the stove hot plates, which appeared someone had spilled something over whilst cooking and did not clean up themselves. The shower could also do with a bit of attention as mould is forming between the wall tiles and shower base. Also to make a good stay a great one, small things like additional towel racks or hooks could be provided to hang your towels after showering. Another small detail was we had no contact with our hosts whatsoever which we found a little unusual. All in all a very nice park in a very beautiful town.
Good value! Nice and clean cabin. Location is just a couple minutes to town.
Lumpy beds and couch, right next to the road which is fairly quiet at night but you can hear the neighbours just walking around in their cabin..no insulation at all.
Great location and owners are good

Upon arrival, we were shocked by the (poor) condition of the park. The negative reviews are warranted and increasing - the park seems on a downward trajectory.

When we tried to set up our tent, we couldn’t get any pegs into the ground—even with heavy-duty equipment. We called the phone number for help (there is no-one on site!) and they seemed surprised by our difficulty, which is baffling given the recent reviews that mention the same thing. The park’s response to one of those was that the customer booked last minute, stating if they booked earlier they would have got a “premium site”. We booked a year in advance and still had an unusable site.
They offered us other non-en-suite sites, but testing other nearby sites we still couldn’t get our pegs into the ground. They denied a refund despite the fact that we couldn’t use the site they booked us into (or any other nearby site).

The couple next door faced the similar issue and left early because they found the inability to set up their caravan annex, and the condition of the amenities, unacceptable.

The park claims to be cheaper than other places. We paid $540 for an en-suite site for 6 nights outside school holidays. There are much better, more affordable options in nicer locations.

We - thankfully - found another park in town at the last minute during the busy Buffalo Stampede weekend. It was a lot better and cheaper! Winning!!  We enjoyed our holiday (thanks to moving to the other park) and will never return to BAP. If you’re planning to camp in Bright, I strongly recommend booking elsewhere (I will post my other experience separately for where I think is a good option). The reviews of BAP - and more importantly, the park’s defensive response to any criticism - speak for themselves!
Stayed at a powered site, no hot water in either of the 2 shower blocks,checked at 10 am and 7pm

I just got back from Caravan Park , and I had a wonderful experience! The showers were clean and just what I needed after a day of exploring. What really stood out for me was the incredible team at reception.
Ps.
The young girl went above and beyond, making me feel so welcomed and taken care of. You can never underestimate the power of amazing reception!
This place has a lovely, cozy vibe, and I can’t wait to return.
Stayed on a powered site. Very polite and helpful. Great camp kitchen.

My husband & I stayed 2nights in our own caravan. Luckily we had our own amenities as the parks were very run down. The ground was rock hard & difficult to place pegs for the awning. Staff , when we saw them not interested in communicating with us.
Others also had difficulty erecting a tent & moved to another park.
There is no dump point.
Would certainly not recommend this park.
Only good point is its location if you want a out of the way location.
Great place

We stayed here for 4 nights over a weekend (Thursday - Monday). Didn't see anyone in charge over the whole visit. An email directed us to our powered site via a site map. Being 4 kilometres away from Bright, it was very quiet, particularly at night. The amenities were old but clean and functional. There is probably better and more expensive accommodation around but there was nothing for the dates we wanted anyway. We had zero problems so we would definitely stay again.
Fantastic place to stay just outside of town. Great staff and beautiful location

I found the bed comfortable,it was a little high to climb into.
The air conditioning was excellent
On the day I arrived it was very hot.
Only negative for me was having to walk up and down the stairs
You might have to check the door lock the lever was a little hard to use to exit the room
Stayed in the Buffalo cabins. A bit dated but the aircon was clod and the room clean and comfy. I would stay there again now worries at all. Very friendly staff.

Won't be staying again.
Parents asked to store their caravan (no people staying) for an extra night after having major car troubles and a lot of headaches/stress and the park wanted to charge them for the night with an otherwise empty park
Very expensive for unpowered site even if it was a long weekend. No water either at site. Amenities getting outdated. Pool and staff were lovely. Hopefully new owners will improve things

Unfortunately not a great park for camping. Our powered tent site was hard dirt and rock. We couldn't get out tent pegs in and we needed to use rocks to keep our tent in place and tie our fly down which obviously moved in the wind and the rain. Our neighbors had the same issue (but this wasn't as much of a big deal for them as they had a van and only needed it for their annex pegs). We heard other people talking at the pool about how they couldn't peg their tent. We asked to move when we arrived but were told there were no other sites. We could clearly see other grassed sites which were empty for the entirety of our stay. The park is noisy at night and there doesn't seem to be any staff driving around. We had people revving their car at 10:30 at night and people in the pool at 12/1am screaming so loudly you could hear them throughout the park.  The amenities were basic but clean enough and good hot water in the ladies (but not the men's) showers. Pool was good for the kids. We left a day early because we didn't want to put up with the noise and the tent moving in the weather any more. We would come back to Bright but would look for a different park.

Great little family getaway that’s affordable and in the most peaceful location. The rooms in the cabins are beautifully presented and facilities on site are great for families to slow down and enjoy being outside. Short trip into town has everything available that you would need but nothing beats the stargazing views that only come from its location. Can’t wait to return again shortly!

What a fantastic place to stay , couldn’t fault it . Staff were friendly, professional and helpful , location was perfect . Facilities were well looked after , We couldn’t fault it , will definitely be back
Our supreme cabin was really nice. Everything was good except the reception lady who was working there. She was so rude  and unprofessional.

the place is needing some love and care. The unit floor needs looking at as the flooring has started to lift from wet weather.
The air conditioner was noisy and the vent was broken and held up by a piece of card. The furniture was stained and needing a steam clean, carpet in the bed area had stains that need cleaning. T.V remote batteries were flat and had degraded in the remote. Some of the cupboards are shutting but just.
I had stayed here a few months back and the cabin was better.
Wish I had taken more photos of these things, maybe something will get done to keep the place neater.
Affordable, had everything you needed for a nights stay in Bright. Will be comming back.

The cabin was just awful - very unclean and smelly. Plates and cutlery still had food marks on them, like they hadn’t been washed properly. Parking spot is very narrow so couldn’t park car next to cabin. The toilet had a urine smell. Very noisy of a night until 3am every night we were there, so we didn’t sleep very well. Awful experience, we come home a day early.
Basic cabins but were clean. Easy to get to, easy check in. Close enough to town.
Nice 👍

Bright Accomodation Park's management and staff were an absolute delight. Very helpful, friendly and hard working!

The park is clean and tidy, and well looked after.

There is a playground for the kids, and room to ride theor bikes around. The pool was also a family favourite for many of the people staying. Lot's of fun and laughing was happening at the pool.

I had originally booked a powered site for 3 nights, but had to change to a cabin (I was not able to take my van on this trip in the end).

So I booked the dorm style cabin and paid the difference from my original booking, but had to reduce the nights to 2. (Because of costs). They were very accommodating and great communication with prompt email replies etc.

Suggestions: I would have liked a mirror in the room somewhere,, even just a small round one. There was a full length in the bathroom facility downstairs, but would have been handy to have a mirror in the room too.

I do have to admit though, I think $120 a night was a bit expensive for what it was. BUT, it is a nice and safe park.

The room was a-bit bright at night for my liking, from the corridor light coming in from the doors edges and gap underneath.

Comfortable bed and pillows. And stoked there was an air-con, as it was quite hot on our first day in Bright. (A flyscreen on the window would have been handy, I couldn't keep the window open because of bugs and misquotes).

The stairs leading up to the dorm cabins could do with some backings underneath, like a sturdy chicken wire or something. They are quite steep and my son tripped walking up at one point, and he could have so easily gone between the steps and fallen down. It was a scary moment in time; but he caught his feet, luckily, and didn't go up or down without holding onto the rail after that. (His 6).

We had a lovely few days in Bright! And I will definitely stay here again, I'll be sure to bring the caravan as originally planned next time though.

This park has an epic space for campers and caravans!

Thanks for having us.
Great spot for a few nights to explore Bright. Would recommend.

I booked on-line and paid. No human contact
I was sent information about my site number and only to contact in case of an emergency.
Powered site was good and level - this is where the positives ends,
NO dump point - no joke
Amenities were never cleaned and hand soap was empty the entire time. Locks on toilets were damaged/faulty so if you did get it to lock, it was uncertain if you were able to get out again.
I didn't even look into the showers as the outside was seriously questionable.
Only bins are at the front gate so if you have rubbish and are in a site toward back of camp then get your walking shoes on.
We knew it was 5kms out of town so that is not an issue.
Obviously, some work is being done on the grounds with new fences and power boxes for sites at the rear of the park.
No one is at the front office whenever I tried.
When I did find a person who was cleaning the rooms I was informed that there is NO DUMP POINT at this caravan park and the one in town was not available - just empty it in the toilet.

Couldnt stay when I discovered the tree tops accommodation required going down the external stairs at night for the communal toilet.  Unable to reflect on the service or facilities due seeking alternative accommodations for safety reasons.  The accomodation looked clean, quiet on first impressions

Rooms were clean.  Bathrooms and toilets were not hygienic and required an urgent deep clean, they were practically un usable. They smelt of sewage and cigarette smoke. Outside the toilets at the playground and bbq smelt of sewage also.
Good service and well behaviour.
Great staff to get all 5 of us in at the last minute. Powered site was great ,lots of wonderful shady trees. Pool great. Had  a wonderful time. Facilities needs some work, but overall was great.

The toilets were always clean, pool lovely, there was some really nice campsites unfortunately as we booked last minute we got a site with rocks and no grass.
So when you book I would try ask for a site that is along the edges of the property they are the nicest. Overall a good place to camp fairly close to town.

Pretty dirty outdated toilets and showers, got emailed the wrong site number so caused a bit of an issue on arrival.
Not many trees or grass just dirt. We went on Nye so granted it was very busy but we were packed in so close we could hear everything including the people on the site next to us snoring all night. Ended up booking an air bnb on the second night! Wish I had something positive to say but sadly even the location wasn't great. Bit too far out to walk but it was a nice ride in on our bikes.
Facilities were run down, camp site was next to noisey road and was hard dirt and tree roots making it very difficult to pitch tent. Found this was not value for money and would not stay again.

We booked an unpowered sight a couple days before heading up to Bright from Melbourne. We have stayed previously but uncounpowered was the only option and such late notice.

Sites were big and comfortable for our camper trailer and 4wd.

Nice and clean sites.

Bathrooms and amenities are what you would expect. Nothing great but nothing bad. Everything you need.

We forgot cutlery thankfully the fully stocked kitchen had extras We could use.

Easy check in process and kids made use of th the pool, park and basketball court.

The room was spacious but living room was too small, basically almost the kitchen. Sofa in the living room was quite uncomfortable, the seats/cushions had no support and basically you were sitting on the frame. The kitchen and toilet were functional. Close to the city centre and although located on the main road but the traffic was not bothersome.

Nice little camp site for our caravan.
No rubbish bins about except in the toilets.
No minimum night stay
Was offered a site right next to a cabin which they were using to throw all their rubbish out. Overall poor service, mediocre environment. The unpowered sites were marginally better with some privacy

My dog & I enjoyed our 5 nights in a dog-friendly cabin.
The bed was SO comfortable; the air-conditioner very efficient; & the cabin was very clean.
I brought several small things to the attention of the lady at Reception when I checked-out :
dripping shower; towel rail kept falling off; & one blind at front door stuck, as it wasn't sitting on the reel properly.

Stayed in one of the supreme cabins with a group of 4 mates whilst on a recent golf trip. Heaps of room and comfortable lounging areas. Had everything we needed and operators are friendly. Highly recommend.
Very neat and clean. Good amount of space to relax in the loungeroom/kitchen area. Very quiet and peaceful. Great natural surrounds. Keen to get back!

We were extremely disappointed to be given two sites where one wasn't usable with tents. It was part rock, part tree roots, and part grass. We ended up putting two tents on one site and using the other for our fire pit and dining. All the while, 4 vacant spots that were all grassed remained vacant for the entirety of the long weekend.
Amenities are very basic, desperately in need of a renovation, but thankfully kept clean.
Sadly, this is another caravan park, run on a shoestring with no face-to-face interaction, no shop to buy an ice cream, no access to emergency supplies or ice or firewood. It really just lacked the holiday vibe we all look forward to when camping.

We stayed in our caravan with our own shower & toilet. We used the amenities toilet on site but our own shower. Would be better if there was more showers then 2 for one side of the caravan park, the pool was a little dirty and we didn’t see many people going around cleaning etc, but other then that was so much fun with the pool, half basket ball area and kids play area. It all needs an update but it does say it’s an old style caravan park. We will definitely stay again, the contact less check it was so easy and all hassle free! Thank you :)

We stayed on a powered site so not sure how rooms are but we thought the park was great, the amenities were good and clean, loved all the hooks in the shower, was so handy, communication was great and then we also got a free upgrade !

Stayed in the Treetop dorm for 3 nights. Room was nice, clean with a good mattress and compact. Would have liked a sink, mirror and bedside lamp as the 2 ceiling mounted flame throwers can’t be dimmed. Walking down the stairs to brush your teeth or take a piss gets old quickly. Communal Kitchen area is ok but only one medium sized fridge/freezer for all and the gas burners bbq could use a service as one is pretty weak. Plenty of kids around and the proximity to the Treetop dorm means you will be hearing them all day and well into the night. There are also yellow bellied honeyeaters that peck your bedroom windows shortly after dawn catching bugs…It’s pretty annoying if you wanted to sleep in. The hosts are aware but don’t know what to do about it. Despite being close to the road this didn’t present an issue. Bathrooms are showing their age but are functional and clean and the showers were excellent. In summary, it’s reasonable fare with some caveats.
3 KMs outside of Town.  Dilapidated facilities. Local lifers turned the park into a construction the entire Melbourne cup weekend
Friendly staff, clean amenities

A quiet, relaxing park.
Showers were terrible though. My husband had a very cold shower - not by choice. Mine was way too hot. In the beginning it was fine, but then got very hot. Although i tried to keep adjusting the taps, I found it impossible to lower the temperature.
Unfair to charge $50 for a powered site then have unsatisfactory showers. I hardly think we used $50 power. Some recompense would be greatly appreciated.

Great accommodation, clean and very tidy, only 5 min from town.
Also thanks for the upgrade, really comfortable.

We stayed in our own caravan on your site. The park was very quiet with no other caravaners there. The park was very clean and it looks like you are in the middle of improving everything. The location was great with just a short ride to towns cafes and restaurants'. We probably wont be returning to Bright but will definitely recommend your park to our friends.
Cozy clean cabin

Ok
Water was not hot, which is what you need after a long day of snowboarding.
Managed to fix by locating the system and opening up the valve for a minute. Seemed to clear the system. So I’m their repair guy now lol
No wifi, what’s with that!!
No soap, shampoo or face washer for the shower which was bizarre.
There was hand soap attached to the wall, but you had to get out of the shower to squirt some on you hand.
You have to take your garbage out yourself.
$200/ night (on special also), you expect the standard items above.
A bit far out of town for my liking.
The cabin we stayed in was quite modern with reverse air and comfy beds so that’s a plus, but for the items mentioned above and the price, I won’t be staying here again.
Beautiful location and nice service. Best place to stay if you are planning to visit Bright or Mt Hotham.

Mmmm. The cabin we had really stunk of stale urine. Of course on contacting the manager by phone, almost a denial to be had that it was urine as they are thoroughly cleaned apparently. No other cabins were available. God, avoid the place. The police made an attendance on our stay, God knows why, probably because it's a crime to call this a worthy accommodation venue! Cabin was dated, stinky and don't dare look in the nooks and crannies as it's grime. There were people making noise in the crappy kids playground in the middle of our 2nd night. Shifty, dirty and dated and not for the wise tourist to Bright.
Ease of getting information to accommodation. Clean, kid friendly space
bad experience never recommends.

All good. Room was great for our needs. It's a little far from town for a alcohol walk home.
Wifi was unusual not to have.
Was perfect for us to drive to from Melbourne on the Friday night, stay in a room with the heater already on for us, then head up the mountain early Saturday. Simple accom but cheap and clean.
Ours was a small cottage with a small room with a kitchen with cook top. + 2 bunk beds. peaceful atmosphere, nice, clean and heated rooms. value for money.

The Deluxe cabin was definitely not what I would describe as deluxe. A warning to families not to book Deluxe cabins. Although clean and beds were comfy enough, the only difference between the cabin and a tent was that it has a roof and 4 walls. The unit that's called a heater, displays 28 degrees, stayed on the entire time, and got to about 10 degrees for the whole 4 days we stayed (outside temps at night got to below -3). The toilet only flushed once in the morning and once at night, we used the salad bowl to fill water from kitchen sink to flush the toilet! The shower only had a drip flow at all directions and could only use hot water. Thank goodness we had family staying else where as the kids got to shower at their unit mid stay. No word back from management when rang and text to explain about the non flushing toilet on night one. There was a communal shower lot in the area behind us, but I preferred a sink wash to avoid getting colder than I already was.
Lucky we had jam packed days at Hotham that we only used the place to go to sleep (and shower, which we couldn't do). So glad I packed our Oodies as they came in handy to keep our heads warm at night!
The $260 per night was way out of line. I wouldn't have booked it if it was any cheaper, thinking I was booking value for money for what we needed. Warm and comfortable nights were all we needed!
The walk into town is great though.

All the reviews here seem to be directed to cabins which I'm sure are true BUT over a week ago my friend and I wanted to stay 2 nights on a powered site and had the most awful experience. Toilets were not clean and the taps were in the wrong position. In the outdoor kitchen - the sink was very dirty so we decided not to do anything. After 1 night we were woken very early with the sound of tree cutting. Apparently arborists had arrived earlier than expected on site and were cutting down trees right next to our van. We were told we had to move and when my friend said he wasn't happy - he was just told - well write a review so - here it is. On further conversation we requested a full refund which was given back and we packed up and moved to a much better caravan park. No customer service at this park so - no recommendation from me.
Stayed here for the weekend with my family of 5. It served its purpose as we weren’t there a lot. However the cabins were not very clean
Nice atmosphere, very warm room, clean toilets

Disappointing and Disproportionate

Our much-anticipated weekend to Bright was marred by a very disappointing display of condescending and inappropriate behavior by the manager.

After having returned to our cabin from spending 1 hour at dinner, we found that our toy poodle was gone.
A quick search of the surroundings failed to find him however as I expanded the search outside, I was shortly met by the manager of the park who had my dog.
Relieved at seeing that my dog was safe, I did not have time to explain the situation before I was berated in such a hostile manner that was not only disproportionate but also had misogynistic traits that made feel very uncomfortable and upset.
Allegedly my dog had been crying hysterically for the entire 60 minutes whilst we had been away – so much so that multiple calls to the park’s office were placed along with other guests complaining about the excessive noise at 6pm that ‘forced’ the manager to enter the cabin and take my dog!
I was then informed that the manager had taken upon himself to proceed with an unauthorized debit of my account of $500 justified as a “bond” - in case the cabin had sustained damage by my 3kg dog!
Please be warned this was an unauthorized transaction that was credited only when common sense prevailed after several attempts.
This whole episode upset me greatly and was compounded when I attempted to verify these claims of my dog’s behavior the next morning with other guests. In checking with the surrounding cabins, they had heard nothing and were surprised to hear what had transpired!
What was meant to be a pleasant weekend sojourn turned very soured after this first night so much so that we cut our stay short.
Caveat emptor !

Skirting Boards had dust on them, stove top wouldn’t work, windows had spider webs around them with spiders and dead spiders as well as dead flies, mice poo on the blankets in the main bedroom cupboard. Was very disappointed with the accommodation for what I paid! Next time I’ll pay extra for the big 4 cause at least I know it’s cleaned properly!

Nice location. Powered campsites were incredibly frustrating for tent camping though. There is a massive layer of big rocks close to the surface all across our campsite and all the adjacent ones that made it incredibly difficult to hammer in pegs. Very frustrating and painful.
Great to have the heater on when we arrived late - thanks
Great location and great amenities

Bright is a beautiful place to visit.  Unfortunately the cabin we stayed in was very old and dated.  It was difficult to keep warm even with the heater on high!  The furniture is very old and in need of repair or replacement. The shower was clean and hot but the shower curtain needs replacing as it had dirt/mould along the bottom.  A little over priced for the state of the cabin.
We were on a camping short stay with family, it was fantastic to be able to have a camp fire, so we could all meet up and chat about our day's adventures..... a very family friendly spot
Beds were super comfy and the campground is located just next to Bright so super convenient to go eat out.
Great price and location. Great communication by host. We stayed over the Autumn Festival weekend and it was a great location.

Fully equipped Camp kitchen, the shared bathroom was clean and the showers hot which was welcomed in the cold weather.
Some nice extras were the hammocks and basketball hoop.

I stayed in the dorm style room, the bed was so comfortable and the room was clean and warm.

The only criticisms I have was there were no extra pillows, some under bed storage would have been great instead of just the raised bed boxes and it would have been so much better with a lamp in the room as the 2 downlights were very bright at night which made it hard to relax at bed time

During our recent stay at Bright Accommodation Park, we were assigned to powered site P34, which we found to be less than ideal for our camping experience. The site is sandwiched between two cabins, and its uneven surface is not ideal for setting up our tent.

Moreover, the adjacent cabin's air conditioning exhauster fan was directed towards our site, which exacerbated the discomfort on an already chilly day. The constant draft from the fan made it particularly unpleasant and is something that future campers should be aware of, especially those intending to stay in tents.

While our overall experience was marred by the issues with site P34, we found the toilet and kitchen facilities to be fair and maintained to a satisfactory standard. The park's location is its redeeming feature, conveniently located just a 5-minute drive from the town of Bright, offering easy access to local attractions.

In conclusion, while we appreciate the amenities and location of Bright Accommodation Park, our stay was somewhat disappointing due to the site-specific issues we encountered. We hope that our feedback will be taken into consideration to improve the experience for future visitors.

Old style caravan park. We stayed in cabins which were nice and they were clean. comfortable for a family of 4. Includes cooking facilities. Camp facilities are pretty basic. No communal common room, swimming pool good condition. Be aware that there is no internet and you can only get Telstra mobile service...No Vodaphone and Optus ?
Great little spot just out of town, old but cleaner than most other parks. Recommended
Bit far out of town for us.
Lovely stay
Quiet during the non snow season
Quiet place to camp,

We booked accommodation thought we were getting a cabin. Ended up staying in tree top cabins. We thought we had better lodgings.
It was clean but shared bathroom and cooking facilities
It say Tree top cabins on the sign at the bottom of the stairs!!!!

Good location 4 min away from township. Cabins are basic . We hired the powerd camping site.not happy about the powered site. They dont have separate sites. Powered sites are between two cabins , where there is no grass and the surface is not even . We had to put the tend on pool of mud, between cabins.
Other facilities are ok. Kids area, pool small shared kitchen , fire pit and all.

facilities-4/5 location 4.5/5 powered sites 2/5

Clean cabin, well equipped, everything works.
Bedding and beds very comfortable. Two very comfortable bucket chairs. TV has all channels. Big fridge.
Great value for the region. Accommodation park not a holiday park.
After day trips spent hiking in numerous spectacular alpine locations nearby, great to chill and crash out at end of the day.

Could not fault the Caravan, it was entirely as expexted and a little more.
The park management are active but not intrusive, which is exactly what I want from any form of management whilst on a break.

We’ve stayed in cabins at Bright Accomodation park a few times now. The cabins are relatively clean (although we did discover mouse droppings - the park attendants were prompt in responding to this) however the provisions in the cabin are pretty basic - no hand towels or face washers, 2 tiny soaps and limited toilet paper (you can ask for more).
The biggest let down is that while we paid a premium price for the Easter weekend, the service provided is all done through a series of emails and text messages. The reception remained closed and the supplies we used to be able to purchase from here (especially sweets and ice creams for kids) we had to get from in town.
Having someone on site to assist guests with check in and other queries would have made our stay a lot better.

We were on a powered site while our daughter was in a tree top cabin. Overall facilities were run down needing maintenance and a face lift. There was a terrible smell near the playground. Communication was minimal and difficult to obtain. Reception was only attended once for a short time during our 5 night stay. Bright Accommodation has great potential but needs an urgent face lift.

We went there for camping 🏕️ and hired an unpowered site for 6 familes. It was an amazing experience overall however the toilets were too far from the site and also were always filthy. They were never cleaned up by the management staff. There was no filtered water available for drinking. The sink was too far to walk for cleaning up your dirty vessels after finishing cooking. The bathroom did not have basic amenities like body wash or shampoo 🧴. The park and the swimming pool was neat and clean however the toilet was smelling everywhere.
Affordable pricing with all basic amenities. Loved over stay here.
The new sites are huge and apparently will become powered sites soon.

The rooms are a bit dated but the team provided lovely service and its cheaper than most places around Bright. Still has essential amenities and a pool so if you want to stay a few nights, this place is a good choice whilst being cheaper.
Staff were very accommodating when we arrived at short notice. Our cabin was very clean and had all the necessary amenities.  I would stay here again. Thank you.
A well managed park in a great location.  We felt the showers could have been cleaner (grimy corners etc).  Other than that it’s hard to fault

Spent two night here and was pleasantly impressed! It met all our needs. Highly recommend for those visiting Bright. The site is quiet, well-maintained, and clean. We had a wonderful, secluded spot and enjoyed every moment. There’s a pool which if you are a family .. your kids will enjoy

The park is in a great location. It has a swimming pool and BBQ.
I stayed in a feather top cabin. It was handy to have a kitchen and air-conditioning.

Awesome weekend away!!
The cabins were so clean and fabulous, beautiful and peaceful.
The pool was a hit with the kids! Highly recommend.. very easy to deal with communication was outstanding.

Only advice I can give is to add a spatula to the utensils.

Everything was exactly as advertised.

Stayed here just for a night, was very pleasantly surprised!

For the price, it delivered on everything we needed.

Would recommend to anyone looking to stay in Bright.
Bathroom, not cleaned, I had to scrub shower etc before we could use it, air-conditioner not efficient, portable air-cooler was needed
Good stay
Cabin and facilities were spotless and management very helpful. Very comfy beds and everything we needed in the cabins. We had a great stay.

I stayed here for two nights over the recent long weekend.

Location is well out of town, just near turn-off to Tawonga Gap, and right next to main road, so lots of traffic noise.

Park appears to be unstaffed, check-in was just by email saying “This is your site.”
No shop or office on-site, no shops within walking distance.

Officially supposed to be quiet time after 10pm, but Both nights there was loud music keeping me awake past midnight, and no staff around to police it.

Facilities are very basic, only one camp kitchen next to toilet block with power points and sink, other two closer to me were just picnic table and BBQ, no power.

Toilet block was basically functional but pretty disgusting, showers didn’t even have anywhere to put your clothes to keep them off the wet floor.

Trying to think of something nice to say… there are lots of lovely trees here, the park is at least nice and green and shady.

Having previously stayed at all 3 camp grounds in town itself, all of which are much MUCH nicer, I’d definitely recommend NOT staying here if you can help it.  Unfortunately I made a last-minute decision to come up this weekend, and the other 3 were all full.
Definitely not coming back here.

The management team were exceptionally helpful and responseive when booking. 3 stars is because my campsite location was incredibly noisy as it was close to the road. Additionally multiple campers were incredibly noisy and children screaming, loud talking, music and yelling and total disrespect for anyone but themselves until 1:30am. Needless to say I didn’t sleep well.. Thank you for your hospitality but I recommend management manage crowd control beyond 10pm which is a typical courtesy time to be quiet in camp grounds. Unfortunately there are some adults in society who can’t mange their behaviour without someone telling them.

A quite, well maintained park. Although the accommodation and facilities were not new, they were clean and functional. The staff (when we saw them) were friendly. We would have appreciated consistent enforcement of the pool rules, our kids were told.the pool was closed and were asked to leave at 8pm however 2 other children swam every night between 9 and 10pm without sanction.
Clean, staff always out and about. Friendly faces. Facilities always spotless! Great views from our campsite!
Great location, good facilities and clean amenities - freshly painted rooms and air conditioning - neighbour had very loud music but not Bright Accommodation Park issue

Very disappointed with the standard of cabin C13.

NEGATIVES
• Run down.
• No ceiling fan or air conditioning in the bedroom, had to use an old portable (provided).
• Sliding doors that divide the cabin up are broken, no one could easily shut them.
• The couch was old and incredibly uncomfortable.
• The three kids bunk bed mattresses were like concrete.
• There was only about 200mm to walk beside the double bed, so small and uncomfortable.
• Front fly screen door wouldn’t lock with key provided.
• Did not see a staff member for the 48hrs we were there. Checked in by ourselves on the Saturday, left key in drop box when we left on the Monday.
• TV was glitchy and about the size of an A3 piece of paper!

POSITIVES
• The location was out of the centre of Bright so, it was much quieter.it is on a main road however.

Hi there - i've been coming here since i was born. Absolutely love it. The new park management are lovely and efficient. My only wish would be for mattress toppers. See you next Jan :-)
Thanks Jane
Loved the location and the staff were friendly and relaxed it made for a great experience. 👍👍

Cabin was as expected, the park was a little dated and no Games area for kids.
Pool was good and close by.
Playground was a due to be replaced as it was quite old

We stayed in one of the Hotham Cabins. It was neat and clean and had the basics expected.
The email updates regarding access plus the key in the door and air con on when we arrived made a great it a stress-free kick off to our mini summer-time break. It was also great that the cabin was in the shade, and the pool and hammocks were super close.
Whilst the double bed was comfy enough, the bunk bed mattresses need replacing - my teenage kids could feel the springs and very little padding. They found this a bit tough going.
It would also be helpful to have a couple more bowls for salads and fruit etc.
Thank you for a pleasant stay.
Thank you for having us at your park!! We thoroughly enjoyed our stay and will certainly be back again soon for another visit!!

Great place to stay as it is more quiet than the centre of town but still close by! Never felt crowded or loud but definitely enough people around that you always felt safe. Rooms were comfortable and very clean which is great for a campsite. Facilities were well kept, maybe a bit of a bigger kitchen space would be nice but looked as though everyone still managed. Would definitely return :)
Toilets and shower are not clean enough and extremely run down. Loud noise and music playing until all hours. Didn’t seem to be any representation from the ownership of any kind. Disappointed

Lovely established accommodation park with beautiful old trees and a pool. The older sites were fairly small and being on the highway, there was occasional noise from passing vehicles. A bit out of town but an easy bike ride or a good walk if you like to do that.

Welcoming and friendly staff. Initial campsite was quite small for a six man tent but lovely staff swapped us to a bigger plot which was ample big enough.
Clean and well kept laundry and bathroom facilities. Kitchen and BBQ area was clean and well stocked.
Some noise from the highway but otherwise peaceful and close to town. Great bike riding paths into town. Despite thunderstorms and rain we loved our camping trip and would come again. Thank you BAP xx

Very disappointed with this place caring more about making money out of the misfortunate than thinking about future clients.
I had a car accident 2 weeks before the bright car weekend so had to cancel our accommodation that they told us was rebooked instantly as there was a waiting list. But after weeks of discussions with the Ombudsman this place would only refund half our money. We tried to use our money to rebook for next year but they would have nothing of that. I understand they have a cancellation policy but if they rent out our room instantly return the money. It’s not hard. Can’t wait for there response.
There reply was what was expected. Money hungry that’s all they are

Absolutely disgusting, I booked a cabin and got a room upstairs in some concrete block, does not remotely look like any of the photos , rang reception because they close at 12 but your not allowed to go there before two and said I am looking for my accommodation as I was looking for a cabin , the most rudest people to be owning a business would not recommend it anybody let alone my dog no one was staying there but says on the website that they only had two cabins left and no one was staying  there just very disappointed with the whole outcome 😡

We booked what we thought was a studio room for $125 a night and on arrival discovered it was a room the size of a cupboard without a bathroom. Didn’t check out the shared bathroom, it didn’t seem to be anywhere close. It was completely unsuitable for our needs. We were denied a refund. The park is miles out of town, dirty, shabby and tired. Absolute rip off!!!!  Luckily we were able to find a motel room right in the centre of town for only $20 more.
We had a wonderful stay at the Bright Accommodation Park cabin. The cabin was clean and only a short distance away from Bright. Highly recommended.

Very disappointed with our stay. Cabins were old and run down. Cabins were not as described on their website. Very small cabin. Shower was dirty. Needed to ask for linen as there was missing linen. Playground was old and run down. Needed work to make safe. Couldnt use powered site as issue with van on day arriving, was not offered a refund or credit. No welcome book on arrival or wifi
Great camping spots under the trees. However, amenities are a little run down and could do with a renovation. Hot rod run weekend was full of noisy men so probably not suited to families.
Quiet site, well maintained and clean. We had a beautiful spot to ourselves and enjoyed every minute. The pool was lovely too. Would recommend and will be back 😀

Not flash, but who needs that when camping , was clean a great spot , friendly staff particularly Michael who was really accommodating for our early arrival

Loved being able to bring our little dog and have a camp fire .

Would definitely return

We had booked to stay here & were unable to get there due to lock downs, so we were issued a credit. The park has since changed hands & the new owners have denied us the use of our credit. When we called to book to use the credit & add additional time, the staff were quite rude in rejecting our credit. Looks like the previous owners valued integrity a bit more.

Please read reviews before you book this property, we didn’t, what a disappointment, booked a so called deluxe cabin, at an extremely overpriced amount. It was no better than a caravan.  It is a very sad looking caravan park.

Horrible place, bedsheets on one bed had blood on them, management accused me of speeding through the park, I definitely was not, they threatened to kick me out via message, kids running/riding around screaming and yelling until very late at night, rooms have very thin walls, can hear neighbours snoring, farting and whatever else They do, way over priced for what it is, nearly went and slept in my car on the side of the road somewhere so I could get some peace and quiet
Stayed in a feathertop cabin. Very comfortable, clean and functional. Very easy check in and out. Definitely recommend

Check-in was smooth and upfront instructions were clear and handy.
The room was kept warm by the time we reached is another plus in the cold weather felt cosy.The rooms were clean and tidy with enough warm blankets and quilts.
Stay, amenities and bedding was ok but felt bit expensive compare to the pricing and what we got. Technically it is single bedroom and innovatively adjusted kids bunk beds in the walkway.
The living room and bath areas are so small, manageable for 1 or 2 days stay at max.
Couple of improvements needed:
1) Sofa in the living room is so old needs replacement, we were getting back pain and was very uncomfortable. Especially middle part of Sofa is not fit for purpose.
2) No soaps or shampoos provided which was surprising
3)Not enough hand towels, no paper towels / tissue boxes.
4) $200 per day for these facilities and such a small accommodation is very pricy.

I highly recommend Bright Accommodation Park. Checking in and out is so much easy and quick. The cabins were clean and so were all the cooking apparatus and all linens. Excellent location close to shop and restaurants. This accommodation park is well looked after and grounds are well-maintained. We will definitely come back here when back in Bright. My family and friends enjoyed our stay. Thank you Bright Accommodation management and staff.
we stayed overnight before heading up to Mt Hotham - the cabin was clean, easy access

Overall setting are good enough and price also fair or even can say better than my expectations. But 1 things need to improve is about the tap water did not clean enough and drinkable. We need to buy distilled water to drink. If this part can improve or provide solution, I will recommend my friend to book the accommodation next time.

We checked in late after the reception already closed. Due to mobile phong issue, we couldn't let the Park know that we would come late. Although, room key was well arranged for us. When we entered, the room was warm with heater on (outside temperature was 2 deg C). Room was clean and cozy. Highly recommended.
A very beautiful and quiet place... I recommend visiting it...
Great stop over on the way to Falls-creek
A good launching place for skiing or biking.
Close to CBD. Reasonable price. Sufficient amenities. Little less spacious rooms because it is a park and not a hotel.

Nice place to stay in, close to bright town,very clean rooms.The owner was very nice as we accidentally book the tree top room which has separate bathroom downstairs and with kids it’s hard in the night.We ask them and they provide us the room with bathroom next night as they were fully booked on the day.
Great holiday park. Kids loved the playground.
Nice and comfy
